info.name: High Roller
info.description: High Roller from The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

element.name: High Roller
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: Fortune is a fickle thing—unless you're a High Roller. These Gunslingers are master card sharps and dice throwers that mix their love of risk with their talent for gunplay. High Rollers push their luck until it runs out, then push harder. Why settle for a win when you could bet it all and win big?

element.name: Level 3: Poker Face
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: You gain proficiency with all Gaming Sets and in one of the following skills of your choice: Deception, Insight, or Perception.

element.name: Level 3: Liar's Dice [Maneuver]
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: When you make a damage roll with a Ranged weapon, you can expend one Risk Die as a Bonus Action and declare it to be a hidden roll. Roll the damage in secret and declare any total you wish. The GM has the option to call your bluff, in which case you reveal the damage dice you rolled. This has different consequences based on whether or not you lied.
The GM Calls Your Bluff; You Lied. The damage you deal is halved.
The GM Calls Your Bluff; You Told the Truth. The damage you deal is doubled.
The GM Doesn't Call Your Bluff. Use the total damage you declared, even if you rolled a different total.

element.name: Liar's Dice
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: When you make a damage roll with a Ranged weapon, you can expend one Risk Die as a Bonus Action and declare it to be a hidden roll. Roll the damage in secret and declare any total you wish. The GM has the option to call your bluff, in which case you reveal the damage dice you rolled. This has different consequences based on whether or not you lied.
The GM Calls Your Bluff; You Lied. The damage you deal is halved.
The GM Calls Your Bluff; You Told the Truth. The damage you deal is doubled.
The GM Doesn't Call Your Bluff. Use the total damage you declared, even if you rolled a different total.

sheet.description: When you make a damage roll with a Ranged weapon, you can declare it to be a hidden roll. Roll the damage in secret and declare any total you wish. The GM has the option to call your bluff, in which case you reveal the damage dice you rolled. This has different consequences based on whether or not you lied. • The GM Calls Your Bluff; You Lied: The damage you deal is halved. • The GM Calls Your Bluff; You Told the Truth: The damage you deal is doubled. • The GM Doesn't Call Your Bluff: Use the total damage you declared, even if you rolled a different total.

element.name: Level 6: Risky Business
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: Once per turn when you make an attack roll against an enemy and the roll doesn't have Disadvantage, you can choose to make the roll with Disadvantage. When you do, you regain one expended Risk Die.

sheet.description: When you make an attack roll against an enemy and the roll doesn't have Disadvantage, you can choose to make the roll with Disadvantage. When you do, you regain one expended Risk Die.

element.name: Level 10: Risk Taker
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: You can use your Maverick Spirit and Skin of Your Teeth maneuvers without expending a Risk Die. When you do so, roll a d6 instead of a Risk Die.

sheet.description: You can use your Maverick Spirit and Skin of Your Teeth maneuvers without expending a Risk Die. When you do so, roll a d6 instead of a Risk Die.

element.name: Level 14: Double or Nothing
element.source: The Gunslinger Class: Valda's Spire of Secrets

description.p: When you score a Critical Hit using a Ranged weapon, you can gamble for a higher result. Roll a d20. If the roll is a 10 or higher, roll all of the attack's damage dice four times and add them together, instead of only two times as normal for a Critical Hit. If you roll a 9 or lower on the d20, the Critical Hit becomes a normal hit.

sheet.description: When you score a Critical Hit using a Ranged weapon, you can gamble for a higher result. Roll a d20. If the roll is a 10 or higher, roll all of the attack's damage dice four times and add them together, instead of only two times as normal for a Critical Hit. If you roll a 9 or lower on the d20, the Critical Hit becomes a normal hit.
